evals.name: init-swift-package
evals.prompt: I just created a new Swift package for a CLI tool that processes markdown files. It has Sources/MarkdownProcessor/ with a main.swift entry point, a Parser module, and a Renderer module. There's also Tests/MarkdownProcessorTests/. I have no docs at all — can you generate documentation for this project? /doc-superpowers init
evals.expected_output: Should generate docs/ with structured directories (architecture/system-overview.md, architecture/diagrams/, specs/README.md + template.md, adr/README.md + template.md, workflows/, guides/getting-started.md, codebase-guide.md, conventions.md, .doc-index.json). Should detect 'application' and 'testing' scopes. Should create or update CLAUDE.md. All generated files should have freshness markers. Should suggest hooks install at the end.

assertions.name: creates-structured-directories
assertions.description: Creates docs/architecture/, docs/specs/, docs/adr/, docs/workflows/, docs/guides/ directories

assertions.name: creates-system-overview
assertions.description: Creates docs/architecture/system-overview.md (not flat docs/architecture.md)

assertions.name: creates-doc-index
assertions.description: Creates docs/.doc-index.json with valid JSON

assertions.name: freshness-markers
assertions.description: All generated .md files in docs/ start with <!-- Generated by doc-superpowers | YYYY-MM-DD | commit: SHORT_HASH -->

assertions.name: detects-application-scope
assertions.description: Detects 'application' scope from Package.swift / Sources/ directory

assertions.name: detects-testing-scope
assertions.description: Detects 'testing' scope from Tests/ directory

assertions.name: suggests-hooks-install
assertions.description: Suggests running /doc-superpowers hooks install after init completes

assertions.name: no-flat-structure
assertions.description: Does NOT create docs/architecture.md (flat) — should be docs/architecture/system-overview.md

evals.name: audit-stale-docs
evals.prompt: I've been refactoring the auth module heavily over the past week. The docs were generated a month ago and I'm worried they're outdated. Can you check? /doc-superpowers audit
evals.expected_output: Should run discovery, call doc-tools.sh check-freshness, dispatch scope agents for affected scopes, produce a severity-ranked report with P0/P1/P2/P3 findings with evidence (exact doc quotes vs code quotes). Should write audit report to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-audit-report.md. Should NOT modify any docs (audit is read-only). Should suggest running /doc-superpowers update.

assertions.name: runs-check-freshness
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh check-freshness as part of the audit

assertions.name: read-only
assertions.description: Does NOT create or edit any files in docs/ except docs/plans/*-audit-report.md

assertions.name: writes-audit-report
assertions.description: Writes audit report to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-audit-report.md

assertions.name: severity-ranked-output
assertions.description: Output includes P0/P1/P2/P3 severity classifications

assertions.name: evidence-based-findings
assertions.description: Findings include evidence — 'Doc says:' and 'Code shows:' quotes

assertions.name: suggests-update
assertions.description: Suggests running /doc-superpowers update to apply fixes

evals.name: update-from-audit
evals.prompt: OK, the audit found 3 stale docs. Let's fix them. /doc-superpowers update
evals.expected_output: Should locate the most recent audit report in docs/plans/*-audit-report.md. Should dispatch scope agents for each stale doc that run gather→plan→execute→diagram→sync. Should update freshness markers on changed files. Should call doc-tools.sh update-index for each updated doc. Should run verification gate (check-freshness confirms docs are current).

assertions.name: reads-audit-report
assertions.description: Reads the most recent docs/plans/*-audit-report.md as input

assertions.name: updates-stale-docs
assertions.description: Modifies the docs identified as stale in the audit report

assertions.name: calls-update-index
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh update-index for each updated doc

assertions.name: runs-verification-gate
assertions.description: Runs doc-tools.sh check-freshness after updates to verify all docs are current

assertions.name: updates-freshness-markers
assertions.description: Updated docs have refreshed freshness markers with current date and commit

evals.name: spec-generate-from-design
evals.prompt: I just finished brainstorming a new feature — there's a design doc at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-user-auth-design.md that covers OAuth flow, session management, and role-based access control. Can you generate formal specs from it? /doc-superpowers spec-generate --design-doc=docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-user-auth-design.md
evals.expected_output: Should parse the design doc, identify AUTH and possibly DATA category domains. Should generate SPEC-AUTH-001-oauth-flow.md, SPEC-AUTH-002-session-management.md, etc. in docs/specs/. Should bootstrap docs/specs/ if it doesn't exist (template.md + README.md). Should set Status: Draft on all specs. Should populate code_refs (best-effort). Should update .doc-index.json. Should append '## Generated Specs' section to the design doc. Should output list of generated spec paths.

assertions.name: creates-spec-files
assertions.description: Creates SPEC-{CAT}-NNN-{slug}.md files in docs/specs/

assertions.name: uses-correct-naming
assertions.description: Spec files follow SPEC-{CAT}-NNN-{slug}.md naming convention

assertions.name: sets-draft-status
assertions.description: All generated specs have Status: Draft

assertions.name: updates-doc-index
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh update-index for each new spec

assertions.name: appends-generated-specs-section
assertions.description: Appends ## Generated Specs section to the design doc with a table of spec paths

assertions.name: idempotent-on-rerun
assertions.description: If run again on the same design doc, should detect existing ## Generated Specs section and only generate new domains

assertions.name: bootstraps-if-needed
assertions.description: Creates docs/specs/template.md and docs/specs/README.md if they don't exist

evals.name: spec-generate-stale-content-scan
evals.prompt: I have a design doc at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-01-pipeline-v2-design.md that replaces our old EdgeTAM sweep detection with a new Gemma-4 on-device model. Several existing specs (SPEC-PIPE-005, SPEC-PIPE-007, SPEC-UI-005) reference sweep mode and EdgeTAM heavily. Run spec-generate on this design doc. /doc-superpowers spec-generate --design-doc=docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-01-pipeline-v2-design.md
evals.expected_output: Should create new spec(s) for the Gemma-4 domain. Should run Step 5b stale content scan on overlapping specs, extracting removal keywords (sweep, EdgeTAM) from the design doc. Should grep existing specs and report a findings table with stale ref counts and severity (HIGH/MEDIUM). Should offer to apply deprecation notices to stale sections. Should append a '## Specs Requiring Updates' section to the design doc with stale ref counts and severity. Should still perform all standard spec-generate steps (index update, CLAUDE.md sync, etc.).

assertions.name: extracts-removal-keywords
assertions.description: Identifies removal/deprecation keywords from design doc (e.g., sweep, EdgeTAM) by scanning section headings with Delete/Remove/Drop/Replace

assertions.name: scans-overlapping-specs
assertions.description: Greps existing overlapping specs for the extracted removal keywords

assertions.name: reports-stale-ref-table
assertions.description: Outputs a table with Spec, Stale Refs count, Severity (HIGH/MEDIUM), and Key Terms columns

assertions.name: classifies-severity
assertions.description: Classifies specs as HIGH (>5 matches) or MEDIUM (1-5 matches)

assertions.name: offers-deprecation-notices
assertions.description: Asks user whether to apply deprecation notices now or defer to spec-inject

assertions.name: appends-specs-requiring-updates
assertions.description: Appends '## Specs Requiring Updates' section to design doc with Stale Refs and Severity columns

evals.name: hooks-install-all
evals.prompt: I want to set up automated doc freshness monitoring. Install all the hooks for me. /doc-superpowers hooks install --all
evals.expected_output: Should install git hooks to .git/hooks/ (pre-commit, post-merge, post-checkout, prepare-commit-msg, pre-push), add Claude Code hook entries to .claude/settings.local.json (PreToolUse + PostToolUse + Stop), and generate GitHub Actions workflows to .github/workflows/ (7 YAML files — 3 shell-based + 4 Claude-powered). Should report counts of installed hooks per tier.

assertions.name: installs-git-hooks
assertions.description: Creates .git/hooks/pre-commit, .git/hooks/post-merge, .git/hooks/post-checkout, .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg, .git/hooks/pre-push

assertions.name: git-hooks-executable
assertions.description: Git hook files are executable (chmod +x)

assertions.name: claude-settings-updated
assertions.description: Adds PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop hook entries to .claude/settings.local.json (NOT .claude/hooks/)

assertions.name: ci-workflows-created
assertions.description: Creates .github/workflows/ with 7 workflow files: doc-freshness-pr.yml, doc-freshness-schedule.yml, doc-index-update.yml, doc-audit-update.yml, doc-review-pr.yml, doc-release.yml, doc-spec-verify.yml

assertions.name: reports-install-counts
assertions.description: Output reports number of hooks installed per tier

assertions.name: idempotent
assertions.description: Running install again doesn't duplicate hooks or overwrite non-doc-superpowers hooks

evals.name: review-pr-scoped
evals.prompt: I'm about to merge a PR that touched src/auth/oauth.py, src/auth/session.py, and tests/test_auth.py. Can you check if any docs need updating before I merge? /doc-superpowers review-pr
evals.expected_output: Should run discovery, identify changed files from PR diff (git diff against base branch), call doc-tools.sh check-freshness scoped to changed paths, map files to affected scopes, dispatch scope agents for each affected scope with isolation, produce review output with P0-P3 findings. Should NOT modify any docs.

assertions.name: identifies-changed-files
assertions.description: Runs git diff --name-only to identify PR changed files

assertions.name: scoped-freshness-check
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh check-freshness scoped to changed paths (not full project)

assertions.name: read-only
assertions.description: Does NOT create or edit any docs — review-pr is read-only like audit

assertions.name: maps-to-scopes
assertions.description: Maps changed files to affected documentation scopes

assertions.name: severity-findings
assertions.description: Output includes P0/P1/P2/P3 severity-ranked findings

evals.name: diagram-regenerate
evals.prompt: I've restructured the API layer significantly. Can you regenerate the architecture diagrams? /doc-superpowers diagram
evals.expected_output: Should find docs containing mermaid code blocks, verify diagram accuracy against current code, check agentic inventory for missing workflow diagrams, regenerate PNGs to co-located diagrams/ dirs (architecture/diagrams/ and workflows/diagrams/). Should flag diagrams where code has diverged.

assertions.name: finds-mermaid-blocks
assertions.description: Searches docs/ for files containing mermaid code blocks

assertions.name: co-located-output
assertions.description: Regenerated PNGs go to docs/architecture/diagrams/ and docs/workflows/diagrams/ (not global docs/diagrams/)

assertions.name: flags-divergence
assertions.description: Reports which diagrams have diverged from current code state

evals.name: sync-index
evals.prompt: I manually added a few doc files and removed one. Can you sync the doc index? /doc-superpowers sync
evals.expected_output: Should call check-freshness to detect drift, investigate doc_modified entries, run optional user scripts if detected, call update-index for verified docs, update specs/README.md and adr/README.md indexes.

assertions.name: runs-check-freshness
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh check-freshness to detect drift

assertions.name: calls-update-index
assertions.description: Calls doc-tools.sh update-index for verified docs

assertions.name: detects-untracked
assertions.description: Reports manually added docs that aren't in the index

evals.name: spec-inject-plan
evals.prompt: I have formal specs at docs/specs/SPEC-AUTH-001-oauth-flow.md and docs/specs/SPEC-DATA-001-user-schema.md, and my implementation plan is at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-15-auth-feature.md. Can you inject spec maintenance tasks into the plan? /doc-superpowers spec-inject --phase=plan --plan=docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-15-auth-feature.md --specs=docs/specs/SPEC-AUTH-001-oauth-flow.md,docs/specs/SPEC-DATA-001-user-schema.md
evals.expected_output: Should read the plan, identify chunk boundaries, append spec update tasks at the end of each chunk, add a spec finalization task in the last chunk. Should NOT modify the specs themselves (only the plan). Tasks should use checkbox syntax.

assertions.name: reads-plan
assertions.description: Reads the plan document to identify chunk boundaries

assertions.name: injects-per-chunk-tasks
assertions.description: Each chunk in the plan gets an appended spec update task with checkbox steps

assertions.name: injects-finalization-task
assertions.description: Last chunk includes a spec finalization task to set all specs to Implemented

assertions.name: preserves-existing-plan
assertions.description: Original plan content is preserved — only new tasks are appended to chunks

evals.name: spec-verify-post-execute
evals.prompt: All implementation tasks are done for the auth feature. Before I merge, can you verify that the specs are all satisfied? /doc-superpowers spec-verify --mode=post-execute --specs=docs/specs/SPEC-AUTH-001-oauth-flow.md,docs/specs/SPEC-DATA-001-user-schema.md --design-doc=docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-user-auth-design.md
evals.expected_output: Should run check-freshness on all specs, check Status fields (all should be Implemented), perform three-way coverage check (design doc → specs → code), produce a compliance report with PASS/FAIL verdict. Report should include summary stats, per-spec details, unresolved items, and a recommendation.

assertions.name: checks-freshness
assertions.description: Runs doc-tools.sh check-freshness against governing specs

assertions.name: checks-status-fields
assertions.description: Verifies all governing specs have Status: Implemented

assertions.name: three-way-coverage
assertions.description: Performs design doc → specs → code coverage check and reports gaps

assertions.name: pass-fail-verdict
assertions.description: Outputs a clear PASS or FAIL verdict

assertions.name: compliance-report-format
assertions.description: Report follows the Spec Compliance Report template from output-templates.md

evals.name: release-draft
evals.prompt: We've landed several fixes and a new feature since the last release. Let's cut a new version. /doc-superpowers release
evals.expected_output: Should parse RELEASE-NOTES.md for latest version and date, determine commit range since last release (via git tag or date fallback), auto-suggest semver bump from conventional commit prefixes, dispatch a drafting agent that reads actual diffs, present draft entry to user for approval, prepend to RELEASE-NOTES.md, sync CLAUDE.md and README.md if needed, and offer to create a git tag.

assertions.name: parses-latest-version
assertions.description: Reads RELEASE-NOTES.md to extract the latest version number and date

assertions.name: determines-commit-range
assertions.description: Uses git tag or date fallback to identify unreleased commits

assertions.name: suggests-version-bump
assertions.description: Auto-suggests MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH bump based on conventional commit prefixes with evidence

assertions.name: dispatches-drafting-agent
assertions.description: Dispatches a general-purpose agent with commit list, diffs, and format exemplar

assertions.name: presents-draft-for-review
assertions.description: Shows the drafted release entry to the user before writing it

assertions.name: prepends-to-release-notes
assertions.description: New version entry is inserted after the header, before the previous version

assertions.name: offers-git-tag
assertions.description: Prompts user to create a git tag for the new version

assertions.name: syncs-claude-readme
assertions.description: Updates CLAUDE.md and README.md if released commits changed commands, key files, or features
